insights.ru: Возраст правителя умеренно связан с политическим возрастом: r=0.331.
insights.en: Ruler age is moderately aligned with political age: r=0.331.

insights.ru: Старение core elite связано с более слабым обновлением за 5 лет: r=-0.347.
insights.en: Aging core elite is linked with weaker 5-year renewal: r=-0.347.

insights.ru: Возраст правителя сильнее совпадает со взвешенным возрастом core elite, чем с простым средним: r=0.505 против r=0.416.
insights.en: Ruler age aligns more strongly with weighted core age than with plain mean age: r=0.505 vs r=0.416.

insights.ru: Число elite-initiated событий связано с максимальной severity: r=0.799. Это текущий событийный сигнал.
insights.en: Elite-initiated event volume is linked with max severity: r=0.799. This is the current event signal.

selection.rationale: Prior match: USA 1789-2026 prefers institutional_domain_crisis_model.; Core elite age is weakly correlated with elite-initiated events.; Event data contains rich decision-domain structure.; Institutional layer is broad enough for an institutional model.
supporting_signals: Core elite age is weakly correlated with elite-initiated events.
Event data contains rich decision-domain structure.
Institutional layer is broad enough for an institutional model.
High-severity events are frequent enough to justify crisis framing.
Weak core elite age / elite-initiated event correlation supports a non-age-centered model.
missing_data_warnings: yearly_context.csv is absent, so party-control and crisis flags are inferred indirectly.
recommended_next_features: yearly_context.csv with party-control, divided-government, and crisis flags
yearly_context.csv for better crisis and control signals
selection.forecast_guidance: Elevated scenario pressure: crisis and domain structure matter more than age. Recent trend context: core_mean_age=62.865, elite_turnover_rate=0.41959756097560974, high_severity_event_rate=0.9382716049382716, security_elite_share=0.17223480558701398.

selected_family.label: Institutional / domain / crisis model
selected_family.description: Events are driven more by institutions, decision domains, party control, crisis context, and policy domains than by average elite age.
selected_family.interpretation_template: In {country}, {period}, average core elite age is not the main explanatory variable. The recommended model is institutional/domain/crisis-based. Events should be analyzed by institution, decision domain, crisis context, and factional-network layers where available.
selected_family.forecast_template: Forecasts for {country}, {period} should emphasize crisis flags, institutional control, decision domains, and factional influence shares. Age variables may be included as weak secondary features.
warnings: Do not overinterpret low age/event correlations.
Forecasts should be scenario-based rather than deterministic.

forecast.baseline_assessment: Elevated scenario pressure: crisis and domain structure matter more than age. Recent trend context: core_mean_age=62.865, elite_turnover_rate=0.41959756097560974, high_severity_event_rate=0.9382716049382716, security_elite_share=0.17223480558701398.
forecast.upside_scenario: Upside: institutional control and crisis containment keep event severity bounded.
forecast.downside_scenario: Downside: crisis concentration, divided control, or security/finance/judicial conflict raises event risk.
warnings: Do not overinterpret low age/event correlations.
Forecasts should be scenario-based rather than deterministic.
